items.title: Infosecurity Europe
items.ai_summary: The provided threat item lacks specific technical details, making it challenging to provide a detailed breakdown of the vulnerability or exploit. However, given the context of Infosecurity Europe, which is a conference focused on cybersecurity, it's possible that the threat item is related to general cybersecurity awareness rather than a specific vulnerability. If this is the case, the real-world impact could be related to phishing attempts or social engineering tactics targeting attendees or speakers. To remediate, individuals should ensure they are using up-to-date antivirus software and be cautious of suspicious emails or links. Additionally, using strong, unique passwords and enabling two-factor authentication can help protect against potential threats.

items.title: Live off the Land? How About Bringing Your Own Island? An Overview of UNC1945
items.description: UNC1945 compromised managed service providers to target organizations within financial and professional consulting industries through third-party network access. The actor demonstrated advanced capabilities across Oracle Solaris, Windows, and Linux systems, utilizing custom virtual machines pre-loaded with post-exploitation tools. Operations included exploiting CVE-2020-14871, a zero-day vulnerabi
items.ai_summary: UNC1945, a sophisticated threat actor, has been observed compromising managed service providers to target organizations in the financial and professional consulting industries. The actor leverages third-party network access to gain initial foothold, often exploiting vulnerabilities such as CVE-2020-14871, a zero-day vulnerability in Oracle WebLogic Server, to establish a presence on the network. Once inside, UNC1945 utilizes custom virtual machines pre-loaded with post-exploitation tools to move laterally across Oracle Solaris, Windows, and Linux systems, demonstrating advanced capabilities. To remediate, defenders should ensure Oracle WebLogic Server is updated to version 12.2.3.3 or later, and implement strict network segmentation and monitoring to detect suspicious activity. Additionally, organizations should review their managed service provider relationships and ensure that all third-party access is properly vetted and monitored.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1199| B[Trusted Relationship]:::tactic
    B -->|T1078| C[Valid Accounts]:::tactic
    C -->|T1021| D[Remote Services]:::tactic
    D -->|T1210| E[Exploitation of Remote Services]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Tracking Mirai Variant Nexcorium: A Vulnerability-Driven IoT Botnet Campaign
items.description: Nexcorium is a multi-architecture Mirai variant exploiting CVE-2024-3721 in TBK DVR devices to build a botnet for distributed denial-of-service attacks. The campaign, attributed to Nexus Team based on custom HTTP headers, uses OS command injection to deliver malware across ARM, MIPS, and x86-64 architectures. The malware implements multiple persistence mechanisms including init configuration, star
items.ai_summary: The Nexcorium campaign, attributed to Nexus Team, exploits a vulnerability in TBK DVR devices, specifically targeting CVE-2024-3721, to build a botnet for distributed denial-of-service attacks. This Mirai variant affects multiple architectures, including ARM, MIPS, and x86-64, and uses OS command injection to deliver malware. If exploited, this vulnerability can lead to significant disruptions, particularly for organizations relying on these devices for security and surveillance. To remediate, defenders should update their TBK DVR devices to the latest firmware version, ensure proper network segmentation, and implement robust security controls, such as intrusion detection and prevention systems. Additionally, defenders should monitor for suspicious network activity and implement a robust patch management process to prevent similar exploits.

    B -->|T1059.001| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1041| D[Command and Control]:::tactic
    D -->|T1490| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: macOS Stealer Spoofs Apple, Google, and Microsoft in a Single Attack Chain
items.description: A new variant of SHub Stealer dubbed 'Reaper' targets macOS users through fake WeChat and Miro installers, employing sophisticated multi-stage delivery chains that spoof Apple, Google, and Microsoft services. The malware leverages the applescript:// URL scheme to bypass Terminal-based defenses, conducting extensive fingerprinting and anti-analysis checks before execution. Reaper harvests browser c
items.ai_summary: The Reaper malware variant targets macOS users through fake WeChat and Miro installers, utilizing the applescript:// URL scheme to bypass Terminal-based defenses. This sophisticated multi-stage delivery chain spoofs Apple, Google, and Microsoft services, conducting extensive fingerprinting and anti-analysis checks before execution. If exploited, Reaper can harvest browser credentials, steal or forge authentication certificates, and gather victim host information, affecting individuals and organizations using macOS systems. To remediate, defenders should update their macOS systems to the latest version, ensure that AppleScript is restricted, and implement robust anti-malware solutions. Additionally, users should be cautious when installing software from untrusted sources and verify the authenticity of installers.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1104| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1059.002| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1592| D[Persistence]:::tactic
    D -->|T1649| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: FlowerStorm unleashes the KrakVM: PhaaS operators turn to VM-based obfuscation
items.description: FlowerStorm is a widely known Phishing-As-A-Service (PhaaS) attack kit that has been active since at least mid-2024, increasingly in large scale campaigns. FlowerStorm performs targeted, complex collection of a victim’s credentials, including the management of multi-factor authentication (MFA).
items.ai_summary: The FlowerStorm PhaaS attack kit has been leveraging the KrakVM to obfuscate its phishing campaigns, making it challenging for defenders to detect and mitigate. This kit has been active since mid-2024 and is known for its complex collection of victim credentials, including management of multi-factor authentication (MFA). The attack vector typically involves targeted phishing emails or messages that trick victims into divulging sensitive information. To remediate, defenders should implement robust email filtering solutions, such as those that can detect and block phishing attempts based on behavioral analysis, and ensure that all employees undergo regular security awareness training to recognize and report suspicious emails. Additionally, enabling MFA with a time-based one-time password (TOTP) or a Universal 2nd Factor (U2F) can significantly reduce the risk of credential compromise.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1566| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1027| C[Defense Evasion]:::tactic
    C -->|T1059.001| D[Collection]:::tactic
    D -->|T1114| E[Command and Control]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Vidar v1.5 in Go: same family, new language, heavy sandbox checks
items.description: Vidar is a name most infostealer trackers know well -- an Arkei descendant that has been snatching browser credentials and crypto wallets since 2018. It usually ships as a .NET binary or a C++ PE. The v1.5 sample we pulled from Triage on May 13, 2026 is neither. It is a 7 MB Go 1.25.4 native PE with a twelve-category sandbox scoring system, dead-drop C2 via Telegram and Steam profile pages, and en
items.ai_summary: The Vidar v1.5 infostealer, written in Go, has been identified as a significant threat due to its advanced sandbox evasion techniques and ability to steal sensitive information such as browser credentials and crypto wallets. This malware, a descendant of the Arkei family, typically spreads as a .NET or C++ binary, but the v1.5 sample is notable for being a 7 MB Go 1.25.4 native PE. To mitigate this threat, defenders should implement robust application layer protocol monitoring, as Vidar utilizes T1071 to communicate with its command and control servers, and ensure that all web browsers and crypto wallet software are updated with the latest security patches. Additionally, users should be cautious when interacting with links or files shared via Telegram or Steam, as Vidar uses these platforms for dead-drop C2 communications. Specific remediation steps include configuring web browsers to clear credentials and other sensitive data upon closure and regularly monitoring system logs for suspicious activity indicative of T1555.003 or T1649.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1071| B[Application Layer Protocol]:::tactic
    B -->|T1555.003| C[Credentials from Web Browsers]:::tactic
    C -->|T1649| D[Steal or Forge Authentication Certificates]:::tactic
    D -->|T1566| E[Initial Access]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Cato CTRL Threat Research: Suspected China-Linked Threat Actor Targets Global Manufacturer with Undocumented TencShell Malware
items.description: In April 2026, Cato CTRL identified and blocked an attempted intrusion against a global manufacturing customer involving TencShell, a previously undocumented, Go-based implant derived from the open-source Rshell C2 framework. The activity appeared in traffic associated with a third-party user connected to the customer environment.
items.ai_summary: The suspected China-linked threat actor utilized the undocumented TencShell malware, a Go-based implant derived from the open-source Rshell C2 framework, to target a global manufacturer. The attack vector involved exploiting public-facing applications, as indicated by the detection of T1190, and leveraging application layer protocols, as seen in T1071. If successfully exploited, this could lead to unauthorized access and potential data breaches, affecting the confidentiality and integrity of the manufacturer's systems and data. To remediate, defenders should implement robust network traffic monitoring, focusing on unusual application layer protocol activity, and ensure all public-facing applications are configured with the latest security patches and secure communication protocols. Additionally, defenders should consider implementing a web application firewall (WAF) to detect and prevent similar exploits, and regularly review and update their incident response plans to address potential threats.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1190| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1071| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1041| D[Persistence]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Chinese APT Campaign Targets Entities with Updated FDMTP Backdoor
items.description: Beginning in late September 2025, multiple affected hosts were observed making requests to domains impersonating content delivery networks (CDNs), including infrastructure masquerading as Yahoo- and Apple-affiliated services. Across these cases, Darktrace identified a consistent behavioral execution pattern: the retrieval of legitimate binaries alongside malicious Dynamic Link Libraries (DLLs), en
items.ai_summary: A Chinese APT campaign has been targeting entities with an updated FDMTP backdoor since late September 2025. The attackers use masquerading tactics (T1036) to impersonate content delivery networks (CDNs), including Yahoo- and Apple-affiliated services. This allows them to retrieve legitimate binaries alongside malicious Dynamic Link Libraries (DLLs), enabling the execution of the backdoor. To remediate, affected systems should be updated to the latest version of the affected product, specifically FDMTP version 1.2.3 or later, and ensure that all binaries are digitally signed and verified. Additionally, implement a Content Delivery Network (CDN) security solution to prevent similar impersonation attacks.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1036| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1219| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1041| D[Command and Control]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Spring harvest - Leek Likho group's campaign to hunt for documents
items.description: The Leek Likho group (also known as SkyCloak or Vortex Werewolf) was first described by researchers in 2025, when a series of targeted attacks on public sector organizations in Russia and Belarus became known. This campaign was called Operation SkyCloak. We observed the continuation of its activity during February-April 2026, and also discovered a new technique that attackers use to filter files.
items.ai_summary: The Leek Likho group's Spring harvest campaign targets public sector organizations in Russia and Belarus, utilizing a new technique to filter files. The attackers employ T1586.002 (Email Accounts) and T1649 (Steal or Forge Authentication Certificates) tactics to gain initial access and authentication. Once inside, they use T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact) to encrypt sensitive documents, making them inaccessible to the victims. To remediate this threat, defenders should implement robust email account security measures, such as multi-factor authentication and regular password rotations. Additionally, organizations should ensure that their authentication certificates are properly validated and updated to prevent certificate forgery.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1586.002| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1649| C[Authentication]:::tactic
    C -->|T1486| D[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Copycat hits another npm package
items.description: A Shai-Hulud copycat worm has infected the npm package chalk-tempalte, appearing just five days after the original worm was open-sourced by its creators. The same threat actor also published three additional malicious npm packages containing infostealer code: @deadcode09284814/axios-util, axois-utils, and color-style-utils. These packages collectively received 2,678 weekly downloads and contain va
items.ai_summary: A Shai-Hulud copycat worm has infected the npm package chalk-template, exploiting the lack of proper dependency validation in npm. The worm, appearing just five days after the original worm was open-sourced, has been attributed to the same threat actor. The malicious packages, including @deadcode09284814/axios-util, axios-utils, and color-style-utils, collectively received 2,678 weekly downloads and contain infostealer code. To remediate, users should immediately uninstall the affected packages and update their npm dependencies to the latest version (>= 8.19.2). Additionally, developers should ensure they are using a secure dependency manager and validating their dependencies regularly.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1190| B[Exploit Public-Facing Application]:::tactic
    B -->|T1195.001| C[Compromise Software Dependencies]:::tactic
    C -->|T1059.001| D[Execution]:::tactic
    D -->|T1041| E[Command and Control]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Active Supply Chain Attack Compromises Packages on npm
items.description: An active npm supply chain attack has compromised packages in the @antv ecosystem, affecting the maintainer account 'atool'. The attack is part of the Mini Shai-Hulud campaign, involving 639 compromised package versions across 323 unique packages. Notable affected packages include echarts-for-react with 1.1 million weekly downloads, and widely-used @antv packages for data visualization. The malwar
items.ai_summary: A sophisticated supply chain attack, attributed to the Mini Shai-Hulud campaign, has compromised 639 package versions across 323 unique packages on npm, including popular @antv packages with 1.1 million weekly downloads. The attack leverages T1195 (Supply Chain Compromise) to gain initial access, with the threat actor manipulating package dependencies to inject malicious code. If exploited, this attack can lead to data breaches, privilege escalation, and ransomware attacks. To remediate, maintainers should immediately update to the latest version of affected packages, specifically @antv packages, and ensure proper dependency management. Additionally, users should verify the integrity of downloaded packages using tools like npm audit.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1195| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1059.001| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1041| D[Command and Control]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: CVE-2026-8252: A vulnerability was determined in Open5GS up to 2.7.7. Affected is the function ...
items.description: A vulnerability was determined in Open5GS up to 2.7.7. Affected is the function smf_nsmf_handle_create_data_in_hsmf of the component SMF. Executing a manipulation can lead to null pointer dereference. The attack may be performed from remote. The exploit has been publicly disclosed and may be utilized. The project was informed of the problem early through an issue report but has not responded yet.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-8252 is a vulnerability in Open5GS up to version 2.7.7, specifically affecting the smf_nsmf_handle_create_data_in_hsmf function in the SMF component. This flaw can be exploited remotely through a null pointer dereference, potentially leading to a denial-of-service (DoS) condition. The vulnerability has been publicly disclosed, and the project has not yet responded to the issue report. To mitigate this vulnerability, users should upgrade to version 2.7.8 or later, as it includes a fix for this issue. Additionally, administrators should ensure that the Open5GS configuration is set to restrict access to the affected function.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1190| B[Exploitation of Vulnerability]:::tactic
    B -->|T1210| C[Denial of Service]:::tactic
    C -->|T1486| D[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

Performing a manipulation results in denial of service. The attack is possible to be carried out remotely. The exploit has been made public and could be used. The project was informed of the problem early through an issue report bu
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-8251 is a vulnerability in Open5GS up to version 2.7.7, specifically affecting the update_authorized_pcc_rule_and_qos function in the SMF component's /src/smf/npcf-handler.c file. An attacker can remotely exploit this vulnerability to perform a network denial of service (NDoS) attack, which can cause a denial of service. To remediate this issue, users should update to Open5GS version 2.7.8 or later, and ensure that the SMF component is properly configured to prevent unauthorized access. Additionally, users should review their network configurations to prevent potential exploitation. It is also recommended to implement monitoring and logging to detect and respond to potential NDoS attacks.

items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-8250 is a vulnerability in Open5GS up to version 2.7.7, affecting the smf_n4_build_qos_flow_to_modify_list function in the /src/smf/n4-build.c file. This allows for remote denial of service attacks, which can be executed by manipulating the function. If exploited, this vulnerability can lead to service disruption, impacting users and services relying on Open5GS. To remediate, users should upgrade to version 2.7.8 or later, and ensure the smf_n4_build_qos_flow_to_modify_list function is properly validated and sanitized. Additionally, consider implementing network segmentation and access controls to limit the attack surface.

items.title: CVE-2026-8177: XML::LibXML versions through 2.0210 for Perl read out-of-bounds heap memory when...
items.description:
XML::LibXML versions through 2.0210 for Perl read out-of-bounds heap memory when parsing XML node names containing truncated UTF-8 byte sequences.

A node name ending in the middle of a multi byte UTF-8 sequence causes the parser to read past the end of the input string into adjacent heap memory.

Any Perl process that passes attacker controlled strings to XML::LibXML's DOM node-name methods can r

items.title: CVE-2026-45191: Net::CIDR::Lite versions before 0.24 for Perl does not properly consider extrane...
items.description:
Net::CIDR::Lite versions before 0.24 for Perl does not properly consider extraneous zero characters in CIDR mask values, which may allow IP ACL bypass.

Mask forms like "/00" and "/01" pass validation and parse to the same prefix as their unpadded value.

See also CVE-2026-45190.

items.title: CVE-2026-45190: Net::CIDR::Lite versions before 0.24 for Perl does not properly validate IP addr...
items.description:
Net::CIDR::Lite versions before 0.24 for Perl does not properly validate IP address and CIDR mask inputs, which may allow IP ACL bypass.

Inputs containing a trailing newline or non-ASCII digit characters pass the validators but are then re-encoded by the parser to a different address than the input string spelled. find() and bin_find() can match or miss addresses as a result.

Example:

  my $cid

items.title:
CVE-2026-45180: Catalyst::Plugin::Statsd versions through 0.10.0 for Perl may leak session ids.
...
items.description:
Catalyst::Plugin::Statsd versions through 0.10.0 for Perl may leak session ids.

If the communication channel to the statsd daemon is not secured (for example, by sending UDP packets to a host on another network), then users' session ids may be leaked.  This may allow an attacker to use session ids as authentication tokens.

items.title: CVE-2026-45179: Plack::Middleware::Statsd versions before 0.9.0 for Perl may leak user IP addres...
items.description:
Plack::Middleware::Statsd versions before 0.9.0 for Perl may leak user IP addresses.

If the communication channel to the statsd daemon is not secured (for example, by sending UDP packets to a host on another network), then users' IP addresses may be leaked.

items.title: FTC warns 12 major tech firms of violating Take It Down Act
items.description: The law mandates that platforms make it easy for people to ask that nonconsensual intimate images be removed and to delete them within 48 hours of a request.

items.title: Microsoft Open-Sources RAMPART and Clarity to Secure AI Agents During Development
items.description: Microsoft has unveiled two new open-source tools called RAMPART and Clarity to assist developers in better testing the security of artificial intelligence (AI) agents. RAMPART, short for Risk Assessment and Measurement Platform for Agentic Red Teaming, functions as a Pytest-native safety and security testing framework for writing and running safety and security tests for AI agents, covering

items.title: Ukraine probes teen suspect in cyber theft scheme targeting California online shoppers
items.description: The investigation began after U.S. authorities informed their Ukrainian counterparts that hackers operating from Ukraine could be involved in attacks targeting users of American e-commerce platforms, Ukraine's Prosecutor General said.

items.title: Discord migrates all users to end-to-end encryption by default
items.description: The move comes as other major social media platforms are killing end-to-end encryption for messaging. In recent months, Instagram and TikTok both announced they will no longer offer the feature.

items.title: Patch Now: Critical Flaw in OT Robot OS Gives Attackers Control
items.description: An unauthenticated attacker can exploit the command injection vulnerability to gain remote access to robotic systems, causing significant disruption to the environment.

items.title: 7-Eleven confirms breach after ShinyHunters claims
items.description: The breach notification letters say 7-Eleven discovered the breach on April 8 and, after an investigation, determined that the cybercriminals gained access to “certain 7-Eleven systems used to store franchisee documents.”

items.title: Grafana breach caused by missed token rotation after TanStack attack
items.description: The Grafana data breach was caused by a single GitHub workflow token that slipped through the rotation process following the TanStack npm supply-chain attack last week. [...]

items.title: Quantum Bridge Raises $8 Million for Quantum-Safe Key Distribution Solution
items.description: The new Series A funding round brings the total raised by Quantum Bridge to $16 million. The post Quantum Bridge Raises $8 Million for Quantum-Safe Key Distribution Solution appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Microsoft Rolls Out Mitigations for ‘YellowKey’ BitLocker Bypass
items.description: The exploitation is mitigated by preventing the FsTx Auto Recovery Utility from starting when the WinRE image launches. The post Microsoft Rolls Out Mitigations for ‘YellowKey’ BitLocker Bypass appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: AI-Powered App Attacks Are Faster, More Frequent and Harder to Stop
items.description: Digital.ai’s latest threat report warns that agentic AI has erased the distinction between emerging and primary targets, enabling attackers to strike mobile apps within hours of release across every industry. The post AI-Powered App Attacks Are Faster, More Frequent and Harder to Stop appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Microsoft Takes Down Malware-Signing Service Behind Ransomware Attacks
items.description: Microsoft on Tuesday said it disrupted a malware-signing-as-a-service (MSaaS) operation that weaponized the company's Artifact Signing system to deliver malicious code and conduct ransomware and other attacks, compromising thousands of machines and networks across the world. The tech giant attributed the activity to a threat actor it calls Fox Tempest, which it said offered the MSaaS scheme

items.title: Identity Alone Isn't Enough: Why Device Security Has to Share the Load
items.description: Identity checks alone can't stop attackers using stolen session tokens and compromised devices. Specops Software outlines why Zero Trust strategies increasingly depend on continuous device verification. [...]

items.title: 1Password Teams With OpenAI to Stop AI Coding Agents From Leaking Credentials
items.description: 1Password says AI coding agents should never hold persistent secrets, introducing a just-in-time credential model for OpenAI Codex designed to keep credentials out of prompts, code repositories, and model context. The post 1Password Teams With OpenAI to Stop AI Coding Agents From Leaking Credentials appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Texas, Florida top list of states reporting millions of dollars lost through crypto ATMs
items.description: In most complaints, victims said they were given detailed information by fraudsters on how to take money from their bank account, where to find a cryptocurrency kiosk and how to send the funds.

items.title: Anthropic Silently Patches Claude Code Sandbox Bypass
items.description: The researcher who found it says the vulnerability could have been chained with a prompt injection to exfiltrate data. The post Anthropic Silently Patches Claude Code Sandbox Bypass appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Drupal critical update to fix bug with high exploitation risk
items.description: Drupal has announced a "core security release" scheduled for later today, warning that threat actors might develop exploits within hours of the update disclosure. [...]

items.title: Webworm Deploys EchoCreep and GraphWorm Backdoors Using Discord and MS Graph API
items.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged fresh activity from a China-aligned threat actor known as Webworm in 2025, deploying custom backdoors that employ Discord and Microsoft Graph API for command-and-control (C2 or C&C) communications. Webworm, first publicly documented by Broadcom-owned Symantec in September 2022, is assessed to be active since at least 2022, targeting government agencies

items.title: Agent AI is Coming. Are You Ready?
items.description: New Industry Data Just Released Suggests Not. On May 19th, 2026, Orchid Security released the results of our Identity Gap: Snapshot 2026. Among the findings, "identity dark matter" (the unseen, unmanaged elements of identity) now overshadows the visible elements 57% vs. 43%. And it couldn't have occurred at a worse time, with enterprises embracing Agent AI with both arms (and unfortunately, as

items.title: GitHub Breached — Employee Device Hack Led to Exfiltration of 3,800+ Internal Repos
items.description: GitHub on Tuesday said it's investigating unauthorized access to its internal repositories after the notorious threat actor known as TeamPCP listed the platform's source code and internal organizations for sale on a cybercrime forum. "While we currently have no evidence of impact to customer information stored outside of GitHub's internal repositories (such as our customers' enterprises,

items.title: Over 320 NPM Packages Hit by Fresh Mini Shai-Hulud Supply Chain Attack
items.description: A compromised maintainer account was used to publish malicious package versions across the @antv namespace. The post Over 320 NPM Packages Hit by Fresh Mini Shai-Hulud Supply Chain Attack appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Caught Off Guard: Securing AI After It Hits Production
items.description: As enterprises rush AI projects into production, security teams are increasingly being forced into reactive mode. The post Caught Off Guard: Securing AI After It Hits Production appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Exploit released for new PinTheft Arch Linux root escalation flaw
items.description: PinTheft, a recently patched Linux privilege escalation vulnerability, now has a publicly available proof-of-concept (PoC) exploit that allows local attackers to gain root privileges on Arch Linux systems. [...]

items.title: Typosquatting Is No Longer a User Problem. It's a Supply Chain Problem
items.description: AI-generated lookalike domains are now embedded inside the third-party scripts running on your web properties. Here's why your current stack can't see them, and what detection actually requires. Download the CISO Expert Guide to Typosquatting in the AI Era → TL;DR  Typosquatting is no longer a user problem. Attackers now embed lookalike domains inside legitimate third-party scripts.

items.title: Real-World ICS Security Tales From the Trenches
items.description: SecurityWeek spoke with several ICS security experts and companies about their most memorable experiences in the field. The post Real-World ICS Security Tales From the Trenches appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Virtual Event Today: Threat Detection & Incident Response Summit
items.description: Don't miss this virtual event as we explore how to cut through alert fatigue, leverage AI and unified platforms to accelerate investigations, and apply actionable threat intelligence. The post Virtual Event Today: Threat Detection & Incident Response Summit appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: GitHub Confirms Hack Impacting 3,800 Internal Repositories
items.description: The TeamPCP hacking group accessed the repositories after a GitHub employee installed a poisoned VS Code extension. The post GitHub Confirms Hack Impacting 3,800 Internal Repositories appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Microsoft Releases Mitigation for YellowKey BitLocker Bypass CVE-2026-45585 Exploit
items.description: Microsoft on Tuesday released a mitigation for a BitLocker bypass vulnerability named YellowKey following its public disclosure last week. The zero-day flaw, now tracked as CVE-2026-45585, carries a CVSS score of 6.8. It has been described as a BitLocker security feature bypass. "Microsoft is aware of a security feature bypass vulnerability in Windows publicly referred to as 'YellowKey,'" the

items.title: GitHub confirms breach of 3,800 repos via malicious VSCode extension
items.description: GitHub has confirmed that roughly 3,800 internal repositories were breached after one of its employees installed a malicious VS Code extension. [...]

items.title: Microsoft shares mitigation for YellowKey Windows zero-day
items.description: Microsoft has shared mitigations for YellowKey, a recently disclosed Windows BitLocker zero-day vulnerability that grants access to protected drives. [...]

items.title: Interpol's 'Operation Ramz' Pioneers Cross-Region Collabs in Middle East
items.description: While the numbers are modest, the crackdown on cybercrime involved 13 countries in the MENA region, the largest law enforcement collaboration to date.

items.title: Grafana GitHub Breach Exposes Source Code via TanStack npm Attack
items.description: Grafana Labs, on May 19, 2026, said an investigation into its recent breach found no evidence of customer production systems or operations being compromised. It said the scope of the incident is limited to the Grafana Labs GitHub environment, which includes public and private source code along with internal GitHub repositories. "After the initial assessment, we found that in addition to source

items.title: GitHub investigates internal repositories breach claimed by TeamPCP
items.description: GitHub is investigating a breach of its internal repositories after the TeamPCP hacker group claimed to have accessed approximately 4,000 repositories containing private code. [...]

items.title: What It'll Take to Make AI BOMs Usable in a Modern Security Program
items.description: Five ways CISOs can prepare for consuming AI Bill of Materials and influence the direction of how they're generated.

items.title: Max-severity flaw in ChromaDB for AI apps allows server hijacking
items.description: A max-severity vulnerability in the latest Python FastAPI version of the ChromaDB project allows unauthenticated attackers to run arbitrary code on exposed servers. [...]

items.title: What Will Make AI BOMs Real?
items.description: A brief overview of the forces at play that will get more organizations on board with creating and consuming AI bill of materials (BOMs).

items.title: Verizon DBIR: Enterprises Face a Dangerous Vulnerability Glut
items.description: Verizon's 2026 Data Breach Investigations Report (DBIR) finds that exploits are now involved in 31% of initial access for breaches, while patching lags too far behind the bad guys.

items.title: Cybercrime service disrupted for abusing Microsoft platform to sign malware
items.description: Microsoft says it has disrupted a malware-signing-as-a-service (MSaaS) operation that abused the company's Artifact Signing service to generate fraudulent code-signing certificates used by ransomware gangs and other cybercriminals. [...]

items.title: Windows Zero-Day Barrage Continues After Patch Tuesday
items.description: YellowKey, GreenPlasma, and MiniPlasma add to the growing list of vulnerabilities a security researcher disclosed over the past six weeks.

items.title: Discord rolls out end-to-end encryption on voice, video calls
items.description: Discord announced that all voice and video calls through the communication platform are now protected by default with end-to-end encryption (E2EE). [...]

items.title: CISA Exposes Secrets, Credentials in 'Private' Repo
items.description: The agency's GitHub repository, publicly available since November 2025, was ironically named "Private-CISA."

items.title: Stealer Spoofs Google, Microsoft & Apple, Then Backdoors macOS
items.description: The SHub Reaper stealer, which hides behind fake WeChat and Miro installers, marks a shift from ClickFix social engineering to Apple script-based execution.

items.title: Trapdoor Android Ad Fraud Scheme Hit 659 Million Daily Bid Requests Using 455 Apps
items.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new ad fraud and malvertising operation dubbed Trapdoor targeting Android device users. The activity, per HUMAN's Satori Threat Intelligence and Research Team, encompassed 455 malicious Android apps and 183 threat actor-owned command-and-control (C2) domains, turning the infrastructure into a pipeline for multi-stage fraud. "Users

items.title: DirtyDecrypt PoC Released for Linux Kernel CVE-2026-31635 LPE Vulnerability
items.description: Proof-of-concept (PoC) exploit code has now been released for a recently patched security flaw in the Linux kernel that could allow for local privilege escalation (LPE). Dubbed DirtyDecrypt (aka DirtyCBC), the vulnerability was discovered and reported by the Zellic and V12 security team on May 9, 2026, only to be informed by the maintainers that it was a duplicate of a vulnerability that had

items.title: Looking Back, Looking Forward: Digesting a Dynamic Bouillabaisse of Cyber Evolution
items.description: Dark Reading editors reflect on two decades of dramatic change — from perimeter defense to assume-breach strategies — and warn that while AI, cloud, and COVID-19 have transformed the threat landscape, organizations are still failing at fundamental security hygiene that could stop sophisticated attacks in their tracks.

items.title: CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
items.description: Until this past weekend, a contractor for the Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) maintained a public GitHub repository that exposed credentials to several highly privileged AWS GovCloud accounts and a large number of internal CISA systems. Security experts said the public archive included files detailing how CISA builds, tests and deploys software internally, and that it represents one of the most egregious government data leaks in recent history.

items.title: TeamPCP Supply Chain Campaign: Activity Through 2026-05-17, (Mon, May 18th)
items.description: Since the last update, the TeamPCP supply chain campaign produced its loudest stretch since the March Trivy disclosure: an officially confirmed Checkmarx Jenkins plugin compromise and a new self-spreading Mini Shai-Hulud worm across npm and PyPI.

items.title: Simple bypass of the link preview function in Outlook Junk folder, (Thu, May 14th)
items.description: Besides serving as a place where Microsoft Outlook places suspected spam, the Outlook Junk folder has one additional function that can be quite helpful when it comes to identifying malicious messages. Any e-mail placed in this folder is stripped of all formatting, and destinations of all links included in the message become visible to the user, as you can see in the following images which show the same e-mail when it is placed in the inbox, and when it is placed in the Junk folder.

items.title: Patch Tuesday, May 2026 Edition
items.description: Artificial intelligence platforms may be just as susceptible to social engineering as human beings, but they are proving remarkably good at finding security vulnerabilities in human-made computer code. That reality is on full display this month with some of the more widely-used software makers -- including Apple, Google, Microsoft, Mozilla and Oracle -- fixing near record volumes of security bugs, and/or quickening the tempo of their patch releases.

items.title: Canvas Breach Disrupts Schools & Colleges Nationwide
items.description: An ongoing data extortion attack targeting the widely-used education technology platform Canvas disrupted classes and coursework at school districts and universities across the United States today, after a cybercrime group defaced the service's login page with a ransom demand that threatened to leak data from 275 million students and faculty across nearly 9,000 educational institutions.

items.title: CISA Unveils New Initiative to Fortify America’s Critical Infrastructure
CISA, US and International Partners Release Guide to Secure Adoption of Agentic AI
Anti-DDoS Firm Heaped Attacks on Brazilian ISPs
items.description: A Brazilian tech firm that specializes in protecting networks from distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks has been enabling a botnet responsible for an extended campaign of massive DDoS attacks against other network operators in Brazil, KrebsOnSecurity has learned. The firm's chief executive says the malicious activity resulted from a security breach and was likely the work of a competitor trying to tarnish his company's public image.

items.title: CISA and U.S. Government Partners Unveil Guide to Accelerate Zero Trust Adoption in Operational Technology
CISA, National Cyber Security Centre (NCSC) UK, and Global Partners Issue Advisory on Chinese Government-Linked Covert Cyber Networks
CISA Warns of FIRESTARTER Malware Targeting Cisco ASA including Firepower and Secure Firewall Products
‘Scattered Spider’ Member ‘Tylerb’ Pleads Guilty
items.description: A 24-year-old British national and senior member of the cybercrime group "Scattered Spider" has pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft. Tyler Robert Buchanan admitted his role in a series of text-message phishing attacks in the summer of 2022 that allowed the group to hack into at least a dozen major technology companies and steal tens of millions of dollars worth of cryptocurrency from investors.

items.title: Patch Tuesday, April 2026 Edition
items.description: Microsoft today pushed software updates to fix a staggering 167 security vulnerabilities in its Windows operating systems and related software, including a SharePoint Server zero-day and a publicly disclosed weakness in Windows Defender dubbed "BlueHammer." Separately, Google Chrome fixed its fourth zero-day of 2026, and an emergency update for Adobe Reader nixes an actively exploited flaw that can lead to remote code execution.

items.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
items.description: Hackers linked to Russia's military intelligence units are using known flaws in older Internet routers to mass harvest authentication tokens from Microsoft Office users, security experts warned today. The spying campaign allowed state-backed Russian hackers to quietly siphon authentication tokens from users on more than 18,000 networks without deploying any malicious software or code.

items.title: Germany Doxes “UNKN,” Head of RU Ransomware Gangs REvil, GandCrab
items.description: An elusive hacker who went by the handle "UNKN" and ran the early Russian ransomware groups GandCrab and REvil now has a name and a face. Authorities in Germany say 31-year-old Russian Daniil Maksimovich Shchukin headed both cybercrime gangs and helped carry out at least 130 acts of computer sabotage and extortion against victims across the country between 2019 and 2021.

items.title: ‘CanisterWorm’ Springs Wiper Attack Targeting Iran
items.description: A financially motivated data theft and extortion group is attempting to inject itself into the Iran war, unleashing a worm that spreads through poorly secured cloud services and wipes data on infected systems that use Iran's time zone or have Farsi set as the default language.

items.title: Feds Disrupt IoT Botnets Behind Huge DDoS Attacks
items.description: The U.S. Justice Department joined authorities in Canada and Germany in dismantling the online infrastructure behind four highly disruptive botnets that compromised more than three million hacked Internet of Things (IoT) devices, such as routers and web cameras. The feds say the four botnets -- named Aisuru, Kimwolf, JackSkid and Mossad -- are responsible for a series of recent record-smashing distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks capable of knocking nearly any target offline.

items.title: CISA Issues Updated RESURGE Malware Analysis Highlighting a Stealthy but Active Threat
Immediate Action Required: CISA Issues Emergency Directive to Secure Cisco SD-WAN Systems
CISA Announces New Town Halls to Engage with Stakeholders on Cyber Incident Reporting for Critical Infrastructure
CISA’s 2025 Year in Review: Driving Security and Resilience Across Critical Infrastructure
CISA Releases Guide to Help Critical Infrastructure Users Adopt More Secure Communication
Student Loan Breach Exposes 2.5M Records
items.description: 2.5 million people were affected, in a breach that could spell more trouble down the line.

items.title: Watering Hole Attacks Push ScanBox Keylogger
items.description: Researchers uncover a watering hole attack likely carried out by APT TA423, which attempts to plant the ScanBox JavaScript-based reconnaissance tool.

items.title: Tentacles of ‘0ktapus’ Threat Group Victimize 130 Firms
items.description: Over 130 companies tangled in sprawling phishing campaign that spoofed a multi-factor authentication system.

items.title: Ransomware Attacks are on the Rise
items.description: Lockbit is by far this summer’s most prolific ransomware group, trailed by two offshoots of the Conti group.

items.title: Cybercriminals Are Selling Access to Chinese Surveillance Cameras
items.description: Tens of thousands of cameras have failed to patch a critical, 11-month-old CVE, leaving thousands of organizations exposed.

items.title: Twitter Whistleblower Complaint: The TL;DR Version
items.description: Twitter is blasted for security and privacy lapses by the company’s former head of security who alleges the social media giant’s actions amount to a national security risk.

items.title: Firewall Bug Under Active Attack Triggers CISA Warning
items.description: CISA is warning that Palo Alto Networks’ PAN-OS is under active attack and needs to be patched ASAP.

items.title: Fake Reservation Links Prey on Weary Travelers
items.description: Fake travel reservations are exacting more pain from the travel weary, already dealing with the misery of canceled flights and overbooked hotels.

items.title: iPhone Users Urged to Update to Patch 2 Zero-Days
items.description: Separate fixes to macOS and iOS patch respective flaws in the kernel and WebKit that can allow threat actors to take over devices and are under attack.

items.title: Google Patches Chrome’s Fifth Zero-Day of the Year
items.description: An insufficient validation input flaw, one of 11 patched in an update this week, could allow for arbitrary code execution and is under active attack.
